description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, crescent-shaped blades extending from either side of a central mounting, each blade having a sharp, curved edge and a pointed tip at the top. The handle is long, straight, and cylindrical, appearing to be made of a dark, metallic or composite material, and is proportionally thick to support the heavy blades. The central mounting where the blades meet the handle is reinforced and slightly wider, suggesting durability and strength. The entire object has a dark, almost black finish, giving it a sleek and formidable appearance. There are no visible engravings, markings, or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian and intimidating aesthetic.
description: A double-headed battle axe with large, curved black blades and a long, straight dark handle. The design is robust, symmetrical, and utilitarian.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the side, showing the thin profile of the blades and the straight handle.
description_view_1: The front view reveals the full double-bladed design, with both crescent-shaped blades and the reinforced central mounting clearly visible.
description_view_2: Another side view, identical to view 0, displaying the slim edge of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_3: A rear view, mirroring the front, showing the symmetrical double-blade and central mounting.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, cylindrical handle made of dark composite or metal, no visible grip features, uniform cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped blades with sharp curved edges and pointed tips, symmetrical, no beard or poll, robust central mounting.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with reinforced central section.
